title: Add M3 Worker headless parity
summary: Prove browser Worker and Node headless run the M3 ordinary-life scenario with matching authoritative hashes and read-only projections.
acceptance: Browser Worker and Node headless consume the same M3 command stream and produce matching authoritative hashes
Read models snapshots deltas and metrics remain read-only projections and cannot mutate world state
Worker parity diagnostics include seed scenario checkpoint hash mismatch tick snapshot size read-model size and latency metrics
Existing M1 and M2 worker-smoke coverage remains intact
No UI React Pixi or Electron layer becomes authoritative and no Worker protocol redesign is introduced without a separate gate

history.details: Created by WM-0045 M3 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.
Implemented M3 Worker/headless parity over existing protocol surfaces; added m3-worker-parity filter, browser Worker smoke coverage, docs and report; required checks pass.
verified: No blocking findings; required checks pass; M3 Worker/browser parity compares six contract checkpoint world/read-model hashes over existing protocol projections.
Independent reviewer verified WM-0058; required checks pass in task worktree; ready to merge to main.

objective: Gate M3 through Worker/headless authoritative parity while keeping all client surfaces read-only.
forbiddenPaths: Worker protocol redesign without separate architecture approval
M4 lamp Chronicle anomaly crisis-chain or director implementation
benchmarkImpact: Records Worker latency snapshot bytes read-model bytes and parity overhead for M3; baseline updates wait for WM-0059.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; client-engineer may inspect read-model usage but cannot own simulation authority.
rollbackModel: Revert M3 parity additions and keep M1/M2 Worker smoke coverage intact.
ownerGate: Prove authoritative hash parity rather than UI visual parity
Keep snapshots deltas read models and metrics read-only
Do not redesign Worker protocol in this task
Block on UI authority platform save schema dependency or M4 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Worker parity and authority boundaries are not suitable for Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings; required checks pass; M3 Worker/browser parity compares six contract checkpoint world/read-model hashes over existing protocol projections.

integration.summary: Independent reviewer verified WM-0058; required checks pass in task worktree; ready to merge to main.
